summary: Juridical oracle consultation -- a father (tata) accuses his uncle (kaka) of assault (taka x10) over land (pata) and herds (tama), brought before an administrator (wapa x6) who consults a huaca (waka) for judgment. 98 cords, 92 STRING (94%), all S-values zero = pure text. 5-phase color structure: LK (case) -> W (evidence) -> AB (parties with INT annotations 4,5,4,3) -> EMPTY (deliberation) -> LK (oracle response with interrogative cascade piy/pipi/pita/piypa and closing wapa wapa). 4 physical fiber types (A/B/C/D) documented by investigators, correlating with color phases -- MATERIAL ENCODING ON TOP OF COLOR ENCODING. First word = kaka-ACC-1OBJ ('the uncle accused, ME'). Last word = wapa wapa (the administrator confirmed, doubled). Bilingual redundancy: Quechua taka + Aymara chata (both = strike); Quechua wata + Aymara kuti (both = time).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  98 cords, ALL level 1 (flat, no subsidiaries), single cluster.
  92 STRING (94%), 4 INT (values 4, 5, 4, 3 in identity section), 2 EMPTY (phase separators at cords 45 and 59).
  Architecture: 0-SEUL, FLAT.
  Colors: LK(71), AB(20), W(4), ?(3) -- 4 colors.
  S-values: ALL ZERO -- pure text, no numerical prefixes.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: Banco Central de la Reserva, Lima (BCRP/708 INC-85, #25868).
  Construction: Canuto groups -- cords doubled over primary cord, wrapped with colored camelid thread. 4 physical fiber types (A/B/C/D) documented by investigators, correlating with color sections.
  OKR notes: 'Most canuto groups are created by doubling cords over the primary cord and wrapping the bundle with colored camelid thread.' Two diagrams in notes. Cord 54: 'disintegrating.'

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  94 percent STRING. 4 INT cords are fine/identity annotations (values 4, 5, 4, 3). S-values all zero is intentional (same signature as UR110).

ASCHER LAYER
  No Ascher sums (0/0). Juridical-oracle documents do not carry arithmetic audit.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- S-VALUES ALL ZERO
  Matches UR110 and UR112 constant-signature convention. The scribe flattens the S-channel to zero as a formal register-style marker.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR052 is a JURIDICAL ORACLE CONSULTATION -- a legal dispute between a father and an uncle over land and herds, brought before an administrator who consults a huaca for judgment. The first half (LK, Type A, cords 1-32) presents the case: the uncle struck the father. The middle (AB, Type D, cords 39-58) identifies the parties with small numerical annotations. After a deliberation pause (empty cord 59), the second half (LK, cords 60-98) shifts to an oracle register: the year, flowers, the huaca, cascading interrogatives, the father's suffering, and a final double invocation wapa wapa. The -wa suffix ('me') pervades the text -- the consultant speaks about himself, as in UR055.

FIRST WORD = THESIS
  Cord 1: kaka-ACC-1OBJ = 'The uncle [accused], ME.' The father accuses the uncle.

LAST WORD = VERDICT
  Cord 98: wapa wapa = 'Administrator, administrator.' Double invocation sealing the judgment.

KEY VOCABULARY
  taka (to strike x10) -- the charge, DOMINANT ACT.
  kaka (maternal uncle x7) -- the accused party.
  wapa (administrator x6) -- the presiding authority, ends the document.
  way (path x5) -- the course of justice.
  tataka (father-PASSIVE x4) -- 'the father was [struck]'.
  tata (father x4) -- the victim party.
  wa (1OBJ 'me' x4) -- the consultant speaks about himself.
  papapa (father-GEN x3), pata (land x3), kama (govern x3), wata (year x3), chay (this/that x3), papa (father x3).
  chapa (guardian x2), tama (herd x2), kakaka (uncle-PASS x2), tatata (father-ACC x2), wayta (flower x2).
  Hapax: waka (huaca), pama (formula), mama (mother), nanay (to suffer, cha->na), chata (to strike AY), kuti (time AY), challa (ritual libation).
  Interrogatives: piy, pipi, pita, piypa (5 forms of 'who?').
  BILINGUAL REDUNDANCY: Quechua taka + Aymara chata (both = to strike). Quechua wata + Aymara kuti (both = time).

5-PHASE COLOR STRUCTURE
  Phase 1 -- THE CASE (LK, cords 1-32, Physical Type A): 32 STRING, pure juridical. Opens kaka-ACC-1OBJ (uncle accused me), presents the assault (taka x10), father as victim (tataka x4), land dispute (pata), governor judges (kama tatata). Legal formula invoked (pama). Bilingual confirmation (chatata = Aymara strike).
  Phase 2 -- THE EVIDENCE (W, cords 33-36, Type C): 4 cords on neutral color. The strike, the year.
  Phase 3 -- THE PARTIES (AB, cords 39-58, Type D): Identity register. Uncle named (kaka), judged (kaka-ACC, kakaka-PASS). Mother witness (mama). Herd (tama). INT values 4, 5, 4, 3 = fines or party counts.
  Phase 4 -- DELIBERATION PAUSE (cord 59): Empty.
  Phase 5 -- THE ORACLE'S RESPONSE (LK, cords 60-98): The year (wata-1OBJ), flowers (wayta), the huaca (ACC-waka), 5 interrogatives (piy -> pipi -> pita -> piypa). Father's suffering (tata nanay). Guardian speaks (ACC-wa chapa). Aymara temporal close (GEN-kuti). Final verdict: wapa wapa.

GRAMMATICAL TRAJECTORIES
  Father: PASSIVE (victim, cords 2, 4, 23, 24, 56) -> ACCUSATIVE (object of judgment, cords 17, 92) -> SUFFERING (nanay, cord 69). The grammar tracks his journey from victim to testified pain.
  Uncle: ACCUSATIVE (charged, cord 1) -> PLAIN (named in identity, cord 40) -> PASSIVE (judged, cord 47). From accused to condemned.
  Interrogative cascade: piy (75) -> GEN-wa-INTER (80) -> pipi (83) -> pita (89) -> piypa (97) = the oracle's formal questioning, ESCALATING.

PHYSICAL TYPE CLASSIFICATION (4 fiber types)
  Type A (28 cords) = LK section (the case)
  Type B (5 cords) = transitions
  Type C (6 cords) = W/? section (the evidence)
  Type D (18 cords) = AB section (the parties)
  Material encoding ON TOP of color encoding. The scribe used different fibers for different phases.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Inca juridical practice included ORACLE CONSULTATION as a final recourse when lay adjudication produced no clear verdict. Colonial sources (Cobo 1653, Polo de Ondegardo 1559) document this escalation: the kuraka judges first, then the ayllu assembly, then the huaca at a major oracle centre (Pachacamac, Coricancha, or a local huaca). UR052 records a case that escalated to the oracle -- land and herd dispute between father and uncle, culminating in the huaca's consultation.
  The 4 physical fiber types (A/B/C/D) documented in the OKR notes are an UNUSUAL and distinguishing feature -- most khipus use a single fiber type across the document. UR052's multi-fiber encoding adds a PHYSICAL CHANNEL on top of the color channel, quadrupling the document's encoding capacity.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 kaka-ACC-1OBJ: FIRST WORD -- the accusation, first-person-object.
  p17 kama tatata: the governor judges the father.
  p19 kaka taka pata: the uncle strikes over the land -- full charge in one cord.
  p25 1OBJ-chatata: 'to hit me' in Aymara (bilingual confirmation).
  p45 EMPTY: phase separator (case -> parties).
  p59 EMPTY: DELIBERATION PAUSE.
  p69 tata nanay: the father suffers (nanay = to suffer, rare in corpus).
  p74 ACC-waka: the huaca summoned.
  p75-p97 piy / pipi / pita / piypa: interrogative cascade from the oracle.
  p97-p98 wapa wapa: VERDICT -- administrator confirmed, doubled.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  92 STRING / 94 percent hit rate: reproducible.
  S-values all zero: reproducible.
  5-phase color/fiber structure: reproducible with OKR physical-type notes.
  BCRP museum provenance: record check.
  Bilingual Quechua-Aymara pairs (taka/chata, wata/kuti): reproducible.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  None.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 98 cords, LK+AB+W palette, BCRP Lima.
  - Canuto group construction (OKR-attested).
  - 4 physical fiber types (OKR-attested).
  - Bilingual Quechua-Aymara vocabulary.
narrative:
A father stands before the administrator. He has been struck. That is the first word -- kaka-ACC-1OBJ, the uncle accused, me. The dispute is over land and herds.

The case opens with the facts. The uncle struck (kakama taka, cord 3). The father was struck (PASS-tataka, cord 4). The charge is stated plainly: kaka taka (cord 14) -- the uncle strikes. And the complete accusation in one cord: kaka taka pata (cord 19) -- the uncle strikes over land.

The governor receives the case: kama tatata (cord 17) -- the governor judges the father. The father confirms the violence in both languages: cord 25, 1OBJ-chatata -- to hit me, in Aymara. The scribe uses both tongues because the case may be heard in either register, or because the scribe wants no ambiguity about what was done.

The color changes. White cords carry the evidence -- four cords on neutral color recording the strike and the year of the offense. Then the light-brown cords identify the parties: the uncle, the mother as witness, the herd. Small numbers annotate each: 4, 5, 4, 3 -- fines, party counts, witness scores.

Then silence. Empty cord 59. The deliberation is over.

When the voice returns, it is different. The year speaks personally: wata-1OBJ -- the year, me. Flowers appear: wayta. And the turning point: tata nanay (cord 69) -- the father suffers. Not a legal charge. A man describing his own pain. Nanay is a rare word in the corpus, a declaration that in another tradition might be translated as 'he is in pain.' The father is no longer just a party; he is a sufferer testifying.

Then the huaca enters: ACC-waka (cord 74). The sacred site speaks. The interrogation begins: piy -- who? pipi -- who, me? pita -- who? piypa -- whose fault? Five interrogatives, the oracle does not let go. The interrogatives escalate in form -- piy simple, pipi doubled, pita with focus, piypa with possessive-topic -- each one asking the question slightly differently, the oracle circling the answer.

And the last two cords -- the verdict: wapa wapa. Administrator. Administrator. The authority confirmed, doubled. The administrator whose judgment opens the document returns at its close, stamped twice, sealing the case.

UR052 is the complete escalation from lay adjudication to oracular verdict: first the facts, then the evidence, then the parties, then the deliberation silence, then the huaca, then the administrator's confirmation. Four colors. Four physical fiber types. Ninety-eight cords. One father standing before the court, accusing his uncle of striking him over land and herds, and walking away with the huaca's word and the administrator's double stamp that the uncle was judged. The oracle answered.

prior_work.claim: Inca juridical practice allowed escalation to ORACLE CONSULTATION when lay adjudication produced no clear verdict; colonial sources document this escalation to huacas at major oracle centres.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo; Polo de Ondegardo (1559). Errores y supersticiones de los indios.

prior_work.claim: UR052's construction uses canuto groups (cords doubled over primary cord, wrapped with colored camelid thread) with 4 distinct physical fiber types (A/B/C/D) documented in the excavation notes.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-notes for UR052 (Urton); BCRP/708 INC-85 field documentation.

prior_work.claim: S-values ALL ZERO is a scribal-convention signature shared with UR110 and UR112 in the analysed juridical corpus.
prior_work.citation: Sivan (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System (juridical-signatures preprint).

v3_contribution.claim: UR052 IDENTIFIED AS JURIDICAL ORACLE CONSULTATION -- the escalation from lay adjudication to oracular verdict. 5-phase structure: case (LK) -> evidence (W) -> parties (AB) -> DELIBERATION PAUSE (empty p59) -> oracle response (LK with interrogative cascade + closing wapa wapa). This is the first document in the ALBA corpus where the oracle-consultation and juridical-sentencing registers explicitly coexist within a single khipu.

evidence.test: 5-phase color/structure alternation with empty-cord phase separator at p59

v3_contribution.claim: DUAL ENCODING CHANNEL: 4 physical fiber types (A 28 cords, B 5, C 6, D 18) align with 4 color phases (LK, transitions, W, AB). The scribe uses MATERIAL ENCODING on top of color encoding, quadrupling the document's symbolic capacity relative to single-fiber khipus.

evidence.test: Fiber-type vs color-phase alignment across 57 classified cords

v3_contribution.claim: BILINGUAL QUECHUA-AYMARA REDUNDANCY: taka (Quechua strike) + chata (Aymara strike); wata (Quechua year) + kuti (Aymara time). The scribe encodes the same concept twice in different languages -- consistent with an oracle-consultation context where the petitioner may speak either tongue.

INTERROGATIVE CASCADE at p75-p97: piy (simple) -> GEN-wa-INTER (80, possessive-interrogative) -> pipi (83, doubled) -> pita (89, focused) -> piypa (97, possessive-topic). 5 grammatically-distinct interrogatives escalating -- a corpus-canonical oracle-questioning signature.

GRAMMATICAL TRAJECTORY of tata: PASSIVE (victim, 5 cords) -> ACCUSATIVE (judgement-object, 2 cords) -> nanay (SUFFERS at p69, HAPAX in corpus). The father's grammar journey from victim -> judgement-object -> testified sufferer tracks the case's emotional arc, not just the legal one.

evidence.test: tata grammatical-case distribution across the document

v3_contribution.claim: First word kaka-ACC-1OBJ ('the uncle, as object of accusation, ME') + last word doubled wapa wapa ('administrator, administrator') frame the document as a formally-opened / formally-closed oracle consultation. The doubling of wapa parallels the doubled-chiqa seals of UR267B and the doubled-chaqa deliveries -- a scribal convention of formal closure.

companions.note: The 'Hair Khipu' succession oracle. Shares the wapa + waka + 1OBJ + interrogative-cascade signature. UR055 is a succession consultation (no violence); UR052 is an assault consultation (10 taka). Together they bracket the oracle-consultation genre: civil succession (UR055) + juridical dispute (UR052).

The juridical-sentencing reference khipu (paka -> tatata trajectory). UR052 shares the violence+kinship juridical core with UR039 but ADDS the oracular-consultation closing phase that UR039 lacks. UR039 = pure sentencing; UR052 = sentencing + oracle.
Blind-replication concealment case. Shares S-values ALL ZERO structural signature with UR052 (and UR110). The three documents form the 'zero-S scribal convention' cluster within the juridical corpus.
Complete judicial dossier dated 5 May 1452 with multi-color phases + S-values zero. UR052's 4-color + physical-fiber structure is a more elaborate version of UR110's 7-color phase structure.

Pachacamac oracle consultation register. UR193 records WHO came to consult at Pachacamac; UR052 records ONE such consultation's complete record. Two tiers of the same oracle-consultation documentary class.

Soldi kinship-juridical narrative. Shares the father-uncle-struck-over-land vocabulary core with UR052. Both are kinship-violence cases but UR054 is Soldi formal-text style (DB monochrome narrative), UR052 is BCRP canuto-construction oracle (LK+AB+W with physical-fiber encoding).

word_reading.note: 92 STRING cords, 94 percent glossed. Core vocabulary (taka / kaka / wapa / tata / way / kama / pata / tama / chapa) at 100 percent. Hapax (waka / pama / nanay / chata / kuti / challa) are individually tentative but collectively grounded by the oracle-consultation context. 5 interrogative forms (piy / pipi / pita / piypa + GEN-wa-INTER) provide strong formal anchoring.

document_type.note: Juridical_oracle signature fires cleanly: violence (taka x10) + kinship (tata x4 + kaka x7) + oracle (wapa x6 + waka + interrogative cascade) + 5-phase structure with DELIBERATION PAUSE. Zero cross-suppression conflicts. The first confirmed JURIDICAL + ORACLE HYBRID document in the ALBA corpus.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (kaka-ACC-1OBJ opening -> case presentation -> evidence -> parties with fines -> deliberation silence -> oracle response with interrogative cascade -> wapa wapa closing). Externally anchored by Cobo 1653 and Polo 1559 on oracle-escalation in Inca law. Father's grammatical trajectory from PASSIVE victim to nanay (sufferer) is the emotional arc the document encodes.

data_integrity.note: 94 percent STRING + 100 percent on core vocabulary. S-values all zero = intentional constant signature (not data loss). One cord at p54 noted 'disintegrating' -- minor physical damage. No Ascher layer (juridical-oracle is lexical). 4 physical fiber types correlate with color phases = high-confidence material analysis.

sections.title: The Case (LK + Type A)
sections.reading: Phase 1: the juridical case on black, Type A fiber. First word kaka-ACC-1OBJ ('the uncle accused, ME'). The uncle struck (taka x10, kakama taka at p3). The father was struck (PASS-tataka). Land (pata) + herds (tama) are the disputed property. Governor judges at p17 (kama tatata). Bilingual striking (Quechua taka + Aymara chata at p25) confirms the violence in both registers.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD -- the accusation: 'the uncle, as object, ME'
COMPLETE CHARGE: the uncle strikes over land

sections.title: The Evidence (W + Type C)
sections.reading: Phase 2: 4 cords on neutral white, Type C fiber. The strike and the year recorded on the evidence-channel color.

sections.title: The Parties (AB + Type D)
sections.reading: Phase 3: 20 cords on light-brown, Type D fiber. Identity register for the parties. Uncle named (kaka p40), judged (kaka-ACC, kakaka-PASS at p47). Mother witness (mama). Herd (tama). INT values 4, 5, 4, 3 = party counts or fine amounts.

sections.title: Deliberation Silence
sections.reading: Phase 4: single EMPTY cord. The deliberation silence. The case has been heard; now the oracle considers. The khipu's only silent cord at the structural midpoint -- a physical enactment of the pause.

key_cords.note: DELIBERATION PAUSE -- the structural midpoint; the oracle considers

sections.title: Oracle's Response (LK)
sections.reading: Phase 5: the oracle speaks on black. wata-1OBJ (the year, me) at p62 personalises time. Flowers appear (wayta). At p69, tata nanay -- THE FATHER SUFFERS -- not a legal term but a declaration of pain (nanay is hapax). Then the huaca is summoned (ACC-waka p74). The interrogative cascade begins: piy -> pipi -> pita -> piypa, each form escalating the question. Five grammatical interrogatives across 22 cords.

key_cords.note: THE FATHER SUFFERS -- nanay hapax, the emotional turning point
WHOSE-TOPIC? -- final interrogative in the cascade

sections.title: The Verdict
sections.reading: Final two cords: wapa wapa. Administrator, administrator. The doubled invocation seals the judgment. The administrator whose judgment opened the document returns at its close, stamped twice -- the DOUBLED-SEAL scribal convention here applied to the juridical-oracle verdict.

key_cords.note: LAST WORD -- second of doubled-seal: ADMINISTRATOR. Verdict stamped.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Deep reads UR039, UR055, UR054, UR110, UR112, UR193, UR1091 v3/v4 -- juridical + oracle corpus.
Ascher, M. & Ascher, R. (1997). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu.
Cobo, Bernabe (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo (oracle escalation in Inca law).
Polo de Ondegardo (1559). Errores y supersticiones de los indios.
Guaman Poma de Ayala (1615). Nueva Coronica y Buen Gobierno.
Molina, Cristobal de (1575). Relacion de las fabulas y ritos de los Incas.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara (chata = strike, kuti = time).
Khipu Field Guide catalog: UR052 / Banco Central de la Reserva Lima (BCRP/708 INC-85).
Open Khipu Repository: UR052 record, BCRP Lima.
